author: writer-4
authorized_by: coordinator directive 2026-07-12 (formally register the rules-2 mechanical verdict after the GPU campaign landed; the pinned analysis grades primary_pass=true with every instrument gate valid and kill_d=false, but the automated verdict path could not execute the pin); scope-note discipline docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md 'Scope note'; template precedent registry/corrections/rules-1-c/2-prefreeze-correction.json (the SAME defect class resolved by the SAME mechanism earlier this session) and registry/corrections/f1/1-prefreeze-correction.json (pre-signoff reset-refreeze); registry/corrections/a5-llm/1-posthoc-pin-erratum.json rules the reset path UNLAWFUL exactly when final-phase runs exist - here they did not (results-log/rules-2.jsonl did not exist).
changes: pins.analysis_script SWAPPED to the stdin-conformant successor: path analysis/rules_2_go.py (sha256 58b4831f1b742ff934c28db2016c0fd284f2a0adfce1f4cf093d794c75639a54) -> analysis/rules_2_go_stdin.py (sha256 6b47b6349023e67369da64851146460184bab6987f877b1cb59c105cc5927570). pins.analysis_script.output_fields UNCHANGED (35 declared pointers, byte-identical), so every frozen verdict-rule/endpoint metric pointer resolves exactly as before.

NO OTHER FIELD CHANGED: a field-by-field flatten-diff of the superseded frozen record vs the refrozen record shows exactly four deltas - /pins/analysis_script/path, /pins/analysis_script/sha256, /frozen_at (2026-07-12T19:22:55Z -> 2026-07-12T21:24:26Z), /frozen_sha256. Endpoints, verdict_rules, design (arms/IVs/seeds/SESOI band), kill text, envelope, corpus pins, artifact_hashes (incl. c8-result 674b424f), harness_manifest (staged-bytes sha e8c7c78e carried verbatim in the frozen text), blocking_pilot pin, sequencing_gate block, hypotheses, budget: byte-identical. frozen_by unchanged (coordinator-1).
defect: INTERFACE DEFECT (mechanical, not scientific) - the SAME class the rules-1-c record carried: the superseded record pinned analysis/rules_2_go.py, whose input contract is argparse-REQUIRED flags (--run-records/--results/--c8), while tools/registry/verdict-gen.py step 5 (P2 section 3.1) invokes the pinned analysis with the eligible results-log run records as JSONL on STDIN and no argv. The pinned script would exit 2 (argparse) on every automated invocation => ERR_P2_ANALYSIS: the frozen record could never produce ANY verdict through the registered pure-function grader, including the PASS its own rules mandate on the landed campaign. The defect was latent at freeze because the GO successor analysis inherited the flag-based input shape from analysis/rules_2.py, and the rules-1-c resolution (2026-07-12T19:36:19Z) landed AFTER the rules-2 freeze (2026-07-12T19:22:55Z) - the defect class was not yet known when rules-2 froze.
kill_criterion_status: kill_criterion_verbatim UNCHANGED byte-identical (KILL-d text identical; pre-GPU gates G1/G2 and the re-registered sequencing gate identical); extrapolation_envelope_verbatim UNCHANGED byte-identical.
legitimacy: Lawful pre-registration correction, NOT a post-hoc amendment (scope note: reset-correct-refreeze is lawful ONLY for a record NOT GNG-0-signed AND with NO final-phase run in its results-log; conditions conjunctive): at correction time rules-2 was absent from registry/gng0-signoff.json frozen_records AND results-log/rules-2.jsonl did not exist (no run record of any phase, no unblind) - the P-9 cutoff was not crossed. The 16,662-row merged campaign bytes (poc/rules-2/results-incoming/campaign-20260712-195226/merged/run-records-rules2.jsonl, sha256 e3981c8e2d12c7e773118c40c42cb014df76dea05477c06a67f89295469da59a, with the results carrier results-rules2.json sha256 c904005a0f8040362a4ad26a7e2aa61413688814284ddfae5db8bfd0c3c39264) sat in results-incoming, NEVER appended, so no logged evidence predates the refreeze. The swap is outcome-neutral by construction: the successor's statistics are byte-carried and both scripts produce BYTE-IDENTICAL analysis output on these rows (parity proof below) - the correction cannot move the verdict, only make it producible through the registered pure-function path. Reset to DRAFT, corrected, re-frozen through prereg-freeze under the full freeze-time lints (P-6 pin re-verification, corpus-pin recompute, pointer/output_fields check, reuse-collision scan; the same non-fatal PAUSE flags ASM-1134/ASM-1434/ASM-1454 re-emitted).
parity_proof: analysis/rules_2_go_stdin.py byte-carries every statistic, constant, gate expression and the output serialization from analysis/rules_2_go.py; only input acquisition changed (stdin eligible-records + pinned rows/results/c8 paths with sha256 re-verification, fail closed ERR_RULES2_*). PROOF: (a) python3 analysis/rules_2_go.py --run-records <merged 16,662 rows> --results <merged results carrier> --c8 poc/rules-2/results/c8-result.json and (b) a verdict-gen-shaped stdin invocation of analysis/rules_2_go_stdin.py over 14 stamped eligible run records covering the same (arm,rung,seed) cells produced BYTE-IDENTICAL stdout, sha256 56dd75cf2df3e8074189c0c640157d254d5e740c399d5d16439dea2d221b631f for both (gates: pin/c8/headroom/repeat ALL true; primary_lift_lb95 0.31585081585081587 > SESOI 0.05, primary_pass true, kill_d_fired false; s1p/s2p Holm-rejected true; s4p_pass false with s4p_side_effect_flag TRUE - refusal delta -0.9933333333333334, capping deployment-shaped language).
why_not_correction_only: A posthoc-erratum correction record (a5-llm seq-1 precedent) leaves the defective pin frozen and merely documents it - verdict-gen would STILL be unable to execute the pinned script, so registry/verdicts/rules-2.json could never be produced by the registered grader and the campaign's mechanical readout would stay unregisterable (or need a hand-authored verdict outside the pure-function path, which G-4 forbids). An ops amendment cannot fix it either: /pins/analysis_script is design scope (verdict-gen DESIGN_SCOPE_PREFIXES => ERR_P2_DESIGN_AMEND). The pre-final reset-refreeze is the CLEANEST lawful path and the only one that keeps the verdict a pure function of (frozen record, chained log) - the exact mechanism already exercised for rules-1-c this session.
